v.CNV: 有一个患病的人，名叫拉撒路，住在伯大尼，就是马利亚和她姊姊马大的村庄。
v.ESV: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
v.GAE: 어떤 병자가 있으니 이는 마리아와 그 자매 마르다의 마을 베다니에 사는 나사로라
v.KJV: Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
v.NASB: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
v.NIV: Now a man named Lazarus was sick. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
v.NRSV: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
v.SAENEW: 한 병자가 있었는데, 그는 마리아와 그의 자매 마르다의 마을 베다니에 사는 나사로였다.
v.WLB: 나사로라고 하는 사람이 병이 들었는데 그는 마리아와 그의 자매 마르다의 마을 베다니에 살고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 这马利亚就是后来用香膏抹主，并且用头发把主的脚擦干的那人；患病的拉撒路是她的兄弟。
v.ESV: It was Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was ill.
v.GAE: 이 마리아는 향유를 주께 붓고 머리털로 주의 발을 닦던 자요 병든 나사로는 그의 오라버니더라
v.KJV: (It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
v.NASB: It was the Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.
v.NIV: (This Mary, whose brother Lazarus now lay sick, was the same one who poured perfume on the Lord and wiped his feet with her hair.)
v.NRSV: Mary was the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair; her brother Lazarus was ill.
v.SAENEW: 마리아는 주님께 향유를 붓고, 자기의 머리털로 주님의 발을 씻은 여자요, 병든 나사로는 그의 오라버니이다.
v.WLB: 이 마리아는 예수께 향유를 붓고 자기 머리털로 예수의 발을 닦아 드린 여인인데 그 오빠 나사로가 병이 든 것입니다.

v.CNV: 姊妹二人派人到耶稣那里去，说：“主啊，你所爱的人病了。”
v.ESV: So the sisters sent to him, saying, “Lord, he whom you love is ill.”
v.GAE: 이에 그 누이들이 예수께 사람을 보내어 이르되 주여 보시옵소서 사랑하시는 자가 병들었나이다 하니
v.KJV: Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
v.NASB: So the sisters sent word to Him, saying, 'Lord, behold, he whom You love is sick.'
v.NIV: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, “Lord, the one you love is sick.”
v.NRSV: So the sisters sent a message to Jesus, “Lord, he whom you love is ill.”
v.SAENEW: 그 누이들이 사람을 예수께로 보내서 말하였다. "주님, 보십시오. 주님께서 사랑하시는 사람이 앓고 있습니다."
v.WLB: 그래서 두 자매는 사람을 예수께 보내어 말했습니다. “주여, 주께서 사랑하시는 사람이 병들었습니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣听见，就说：“这病不至于死，而是为了　神的荣耀，使　神的儿子因此得到荣耀。”
v.ESV: But when Jesus heard it he said, “This illness does not lead to death. It is for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 들으시고 이르시되 이 병은 죽을 병이 아니라 하나님의 영광을 위함이요 하나님의 아들이 이로 말미암아 영광을 받게 하려 함이라 하시더라
v.KJV: When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.
v.NASB: But when Jesus heard this, He said, 'This sickness is not to end in death, but for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified by it.'
v.NIV: When he heard this, Jesus said, “This sickness will not end in death. No, it is for God’s glory so that God’s Son may be glorified through it.”
v.NRSV: But when Jesus heard it, he said, “This illness does not lead to death; rather it is for God’s glory, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 들으시고 말씀하셨다. "이 병은, 죽을 병이 아니라 오히려 하나님의 영광을 드러낼 병이다. 이것으로 말미암아 하나님의 아들이 영광을 받게 될 것이다."
v.WLB: 예수께서는 이 말을 듣고 말씀하셨습니다. “이 병은 죽을 병이 아니다. 이것은 하나님의 영광을 위한 것이요, 이 일을 통해 하나님의 아들이 영광을 받게 될 것이다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣向来爱马大和她的妹妹马利亚，以及拉撒路。
v.ESV: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
v.GAE: 예수께서 본래 마르다와 그 동생과 나사로를 사랑하시더니
v.KJV: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
v.NASB: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
v.NIV: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
v.NRSV: Accordingly, though Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus,
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 마르다와 그의 자매와 나사로를 사랑하셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 마르다와 그녀의 자매와 나사로를 사랑하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他听说拉撒路病了，仍然在原来的地方住了两天，
v.ESV: So, when he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.
v.GAE: 나사로가 병들었다 함을 들으시고 그 계시던 곳에 이틀을 더 유하시고
v.KJV: When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
v.NASB: So when He heard that he was sick, He then stayed two days longer in the place where He was.
v.NIV: So when he heard that Lazarus was sick, he stayed where he was two more days,
v.NRSV: after having heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 예수께서는 나사로가 앓는다는 말을 들으시고도, 계시던 그 곳에 이틀이나 더 머무르셨다.
v.WLB: 그러나 나사로가 아프다는 말을 들으시고도 예수께서는 계시던 곳에 이틀이나 더 머무르셨습니다.

v.CNV: 然后对门徒说：“我们再到犹太去吧。”
v.ESV: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
v.GAE: 그 후에 제자들에게 이르시되 유대로 다시 가자 하시니
v.KJV: Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.
v.NASB: Then after this He said to the disciples, 'Let us go to Judea again.'
v.NIV: and then he said to his disciples, “Let us go back to Judea.”
v.NRSV: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
v.SAENEW: 그리고 나서 제자들에게 "다시 유대 지방으로 가자" 하고 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서야 예수께서 제자들에게 “다시 유대 지방으로 돌아가자” 하고 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 门徒对他说：“拉比，近来犹太人要拿石头打你，你还到那里去吗？”
v.ESV: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now seeking to stone you, and are you going there again?”
v.GAE: 제자들이 말하되 랍비여 방금도 유대인들이 돌로 치려 하였는데 또 그리로 가시려 하나이까
v.KJV: His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
v.NASB: The disciples said to Him, 'Rabbi, the Jews were just now seeking to stone You, and are You going there again?'
v.NIV: “But Rabbi,” they said, “a short while ago the Jews there tried to stone you, and yet you are going back?”
v.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now trying to stone you, and are you going there again?”
v.SAENEW: 제자들이 예수께 말하였다. "선생님, 방금도 유대 사람들이 선생님을 돌로 치려고 하였는데, 다시 그리로 가려고 하십니까?"
v.WLB: 제자들이 예수께 말했습니다. “랍비여, 얼마 전에 유대 사람들이 선생님을 돌로 치려고 했는데 또다시 그리로 가려고 하십니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“白昼不是有十二小时吗？人若在白昼行走，就不会跌倒，因为他看见这世上的光；
v.ESV: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하시되 낮이 열두 시간이 아니냐 사람이 낮에 다니면 이 세상의 빛을 보므로 실족하지 아니하고
v.KJV: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
v.NASB: Jesus answered, 'Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
v.NIV: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours of daylight? Anyone who walks in the daytime will not stumble, for they see by this world’s light.
v.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours of daylight? Those who walk during the day do not stumble, because they see the light of this world.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 대답하셨다. "낮은 열두 시간이나 되지 않느냐? 사람이 낮에 걸어다니면, 햇빛이 있으므로 걸려서 넘어지지 않는다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “낮은 12시간이나 되지 않느냐? 낮에 다니는 사람은 이 세상의 빛을 보기 때문에 넘어지지 않는다.

v.CNV: 人若在夜间行走，就会跌倒，因为他没有光。”
v.ESV: But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
v.GAE: 밤에 다니면 빛이 그 사람 안에 없는 고로 실족하느니라
v.KJV: But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
v.NASB: 'But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.'
v.NIV: It is when a person walks at night that they stumble, for they have no light.”
v.NRSV: But those who walk at night stumble, because the light is not in them.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 밤에 걸어다니면, 빛이 그 사람 안에 없으므로, 걸려서 넘어진다."
v.WLB: 그러나 밤에 다니면 그 사람 안에 빛이 없기 때문에 넘어진다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣说完了这些话，跟着又对他们说：“我们的朋友拉撒路睡了，我要去唤醒他。”
v.ESV: After saying these things, he said to them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I go to awaken him.”
v.GAE: 이 말씀을 하신 후에 또 이르시되 우리 친구 나사로가 잠들었도다 그러나 내가 깨우러 가노라
v.KJV: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.
v.NASB: This He said, and after that He said to them, 'Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I go, so that I may awaken him out of sleep.'
v.NIV: After he had said this, he went on to tell them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I am going there to wake him up.”
v.NRSV: After saying this, he told them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I am going there to awaken him.”
v.SAENEW: 이 말씀을 하신 뒤에, 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "우리 친구 나사로는 잠들었다. 내가 가서, 그를 깨우겠다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 이 말씀을 하신 뒤에 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “우리 친구 나사로는 잠이 들었다. 그러나 이제 내가 가서 그를 깨우겠다.”

v.CNV: 门徒说：“主啊，如果他睡了，就会好过来的。”
v.ESV: The disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”
v.GAE: 제자들이 이르되 주여 잠들었으면 낫겠나이다 하더라
v.KJV: Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
v.NASB: The disciples then said to Him, 'Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.'
v.NIV: His disciples replied, “Lord, if he sleeps, he will get better.”
v.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will be all right.”
v.SAENEW: 제자들이 말하였다. "주님, 그가 잠들었으면, 낫게 될 것입니다."
v.WLB: 예수의 제자들이 대답했습니다. “주여, 잠들었다면 낫게 될 것입니다.”

v.CNV: 其实耶稣是指着拉撒路的死说的，门徒却以为他是指正常的睡眠说的。
v.ESV: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he meant taking rest in sleep.
v.GAE: 예수는 그의 죽음을 가리켜 말씀하신 것이나 그들은 잠들어 쉬는 것을 가리켜 말씀하심인 줄 생각하는지라
v.KJV: Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
v.NASB: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that He was speaking of literal sleep.
v.NIV: Jesus had been speaking of his death, but his disciples thought he meant natural sleep.
v.NRSV: Jesus, however, had been speaking about his death, but they thought that he was referring merely to sleep.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 나사로가 죽었다는 뜻으로 말씀하셨는데, 제자들은 그가 잠이 들어 쉰다고 말씀하시는 것으로 생각하였다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 나사로의 죽음을 가리켜 말씀하신 것인데 제자들은 말 그대로 잠들었다고 생각한 것입니다.

v.CNV: 于是，耶稣公开地告诉他们：“拉撒路死了。
v.ESV: Then Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus has died,
v.GAE: 이에 예수께서 밝히 이르시되 나사로가 죽었느니라
v.KJV: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
v.NASB: So Jesus then said to them plainly, 'Lazarus is dead,
v.NIV: So then he told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead,
v.NRSV: Then Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead.
v.SAENEW: 이 때에 예수께서 그들에게 밝혀 말씀하셨다. "나사로는 죽었다.
v.WLB: 그래서 예수께서는 그들에게 분명히 말씀해 주셨습니다. “나사로는 죽었다.

v.CNV: 我为你们欢喜，因为我不在那里，是要使你们相信。现在我们到他那里去吧。”
v.ESV: and for your sake I am glad that I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
v.GAE: 내가 거기 있지 아니한 것을 너희를 위하여 기뻐하노니 이는 너희로 믿게 하려 함이라 그러나 그에게로 가자 하시니
v.KJV: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
v.NASB: and I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe; but let us go to him.'
v.NIV: and for your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
v.NRSV: For your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
v.SAENEW: 내가 거기에 있지 않은 것이 너희를 위해서 도리어 잘 된 일이므로, 기쁘게 생각한다. 이 일로 말미암아 너희가 믿게 될 것이다. 그에게로 가자."
v.WLB: 내가 거기 있지 않은 것을 기뻐하는 까닭은 너희를 위해서다. 이 일로 인해 너희가 믿게 될 것이다. 이제 나사로에게로 가자.”

v.CNV: 那称为“双生子”的多马，对其他的门徒说：“我们也去跟他一同死吧！”
v.ESV: So Thomas, called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
v.GAE: 디두모라고도 하는 도마가 다른 제자들에게 말하되 우리도 주와 함께 죽으러 가자 하니라
v.KJV: Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.
v.NASB: Therefore Thomas, who is called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, 'Let us also go, so that we may die with Him.'
v.NIV: Then Thomas (also known as Didymus ) said to the rest of the disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
v.NRSV: Thomas, who was called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 디두모라고도 하는 도마가 동료 제자들에게 "우리도 그와 함께 죽으러 가자" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 디두모라고도 하는 도마가 다른 제자들에게 말했습니다. “우리도 주와 함께 죽으러 가자.”

v.CNV: 耶稣到了，知道拉撒路在坟墓里已经四天了。
v.ESV: Now when Jesus came, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
v.GAE: 예수께서 와서 보시니 나사로가 무덤에 있은 지 이미 나흘이라
v.KJV: Then when Jesus came, he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.
v.NASB: So when Jesus came, He found that he had already been in the tomb four days.
v.NIV: On his arrival, Jesus found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
v.NRSV: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 가서 보시니, 나사로가 무덤 속에 있은 지가 벌써 나흘이나 되었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그곳에 도착하셔서 보니, 나사로가 무덤 속에 있은 지 이미 4일이나 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 伯大尼靠近耶路撒冷，相距约有三公里。
v.ESV: Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles off,
v.GAE: 베다니는 예루살렘에서 가깝기가 한 오 리쯤 되매
v.KJV: Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
v.NASB: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles off;
v.NIV: Now Bethany was less than two miles from Jerusalem,
v.NRSV: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, some two miles away,
v.SAENEW: 베다니는 예루살렘에서 오 리가 조금 넘는 가까운 곳인데,
v.WLB: 베다니는 예루살렘에서 약 15스타디온 못 미치는 곳에 있었기 때문에

v.CNV: 有许多犹太人来到马大和马利亚那里，为了拉撒路的死来安慰她们。
v.ESV: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them concerning their brother.
v.GAE: 많은 유대인이 마르다와 마리아에게 그 오라비의 일로 위문하러 왔더니
v.KJV: And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
v.NASB: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary, to console them concerning their brother.

v.NRSV: and not for the nation only, but to gather into one the dispersed children of God.
v.SAENEW: 민족을 위할 뿐만 아니라, 흩어져 있는 하나님의 자녀를 한데 모아서 하나가 되게 하기 위하여 죽으실 것을 예언한 것이다.
v.WLB: 또한 유대 민족뿐만 아니라 흩어진 하나님의 자녀들을 모아 하나 되게 하기 위해 죽으실 것을 예언한 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 从那天起，他们就想杀害耶稣。
v.ESV: So from that day on they made plans to put him to death.
v.GAE: 이 날부터는 그들이 예수를 죽이려고 모의하니라
v.KJV: Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.
v.NASB: So from that day on they planned together to kill Him.
v.NIV: So from that day on they plotted to take his life.
v.NRSV: So from that day on they planned to put him to death.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 그 날로부터 예수를 죽이려고 모의하였다.
v.WLB: 그날로부터 그들은 예수를 죽이려고 음모를 꾸몄습니다.

v.CNV: 因此，耶稣不再在犹太人中间公开活动，却离开那里，到旷野附近的地方去；到了一座名叫以法莲的城，就和门徒住在那里。
v.ESV: Jesus therefore no longer walked openly among the Jews, but went from there to the region near the wilderness, to a town called Ephraim, and there he stayed with the disciples.
v.GAE: 그러므로 예수께서 다시 유대인 가운데 드러나게 다니지 아니하시고 거기를 떠나 빈 들 가까운 곳인 에브라임이라는 동네에 가서 제자들과 함께 거기 머무르시니라
v.KJV: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
v.NASB: Therefore Jesus no longer continued to walk publicly among the Jews, but went away from there to the country near the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim; and there He stayed with the disciples.
v.NIV: Therefore Jesus no longer moved about publicly among the people of Judea. Instead he withdrew to a region near the wilderness, to a village called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
v.NRSV: Jesus therefore no longer walked about openly among the Jews, but went from there to a town called Ephraim in the region near the wilderness; and he remained there with the disciples.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 예수께서는 유대 사람들 가운데로 더 이상 드러나게 다니지 아니하시고, 거기에서 떠나, 광야에서 가까운 지방 에브라임이라는 마을로 가서, 제자들과 함께 지내셨다.
v.WLB: 그래서 예수께서는 유대 사람들 가운데 더 이상 드러나게 다니지 않으셨습니다. 거기에서 떠나 광야 가까이에 있는 에브라임이라는 마을로 가서 제자들과 함께 머무르셨습니다.

v.CNV: 犹太人的逾越节快到了，有许多人在过节前从各乡上耶路撒冷去，要在那里洁净自己。
v.ESV: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
v.GAE: 유대인의 유월절이 가까우매 많은 사람이 자기를 성결하게 하기 위하여 유월절 전에 시골에서 예루살렘으로 올라갔더니
v.KJV: And the Jews’ passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.
v.NASB: Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up to Jerusalem out of the country before the Passover to purify themselves.
v.NIV: When it was almost time for the Jewish Passover, many went up from the country to Jerusalem for their ceremonial cleansing before the Passover.
v.NRSV: Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
v.SAENEW: 유대 사람들의 유월절이 가까이 다가오니, 많은 사람이 자기의 몸을 성결하게 하려고, 유월절 전에 시골에서 예루살렘으로 올라왔다.
v.WLB: 유대 사람의 유월절이 다가오자 많은 사람들이 유월절이 되기도 전에 자신의 몸을 정결하게 하려고 시골로부터 예루살렘에 올라왔습니다.

v.CNV: 他们到处找寻耶稣，站在圣殿里的时候，彼此说：“你们认为怎样？他不会来过节吧？”
v.ESV: They were looking for Jesus and saying to one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? That he will not come to the feast at all?”
v.GAE: 그들이 예수를 찾으며 성전에 서서 서로 말하되 너희 생각에는 어떠하냐 그가 명절에 오지 아니하겠느냐 하니
v.KJV: Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
v.NASB: So they were seeking for Jesus, and were saying to one another as they stood in the temple, 'What do you think; that He will not come to the feast at all?'
v.NIV: They kept looking for Jesus, and as they stood in the temple courts they asked one another, “What do you think? Isn’t he coming to the festival at all?”
v.NRSV: They were looking for Jesus and were asking one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? Surely he will not come to the festival, will he?”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 예수를 찾다가, 성전 뜰에 서서 서로 말하였다. "당신들은 어떻게 생각합니까? 그가 명절을 지키러 오지 않겠습니까?"
v.WLB: 사람들은 예수를 찾으면서 성전에 서서 서로 말했습니다. “어떻게 생각하시오? 그분이 유월절에 오시지 않겠소?”

v.CNV: 祭司长和法利赛人早已下了命令：如果有人知道耶稣在哪里，就要前来报告，好去逮捕他。
v.ESV: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where he was, he should let them know, so that they might arrest him.
v.GAE: 이는 대제사장들과 바리새인들이 누구든지 예수 있는 곳을 알거든 신고하여 잡게 하라 명령하였음이러라
v.KJV: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.
v.NASB: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where He was, he was to report it, so that they might seize Him.
v.NIV: But the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that anyone who found out where Jesus was should report it so that they might arrest him.
v.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where Jesus was should let them know, so that they might arrest him.
v.SAENEW: 대제사장들과 바리새파 사람들은 예수를 잡으려고, 누구든지 그가 있는 곳을 알거든 알려 달라는 명령을 내려 두었다.
v.WLB: 그러나 대제사장들과 바리새파 사람들은 예수를 붙잡으려고 누구든지 예수께서 계신 곳을 알면 반드시 자기들에게 알려야 한다는 명령을 내렸습니다.
